title: Cold-Start Label Budget: Pool-Based Active-Learning Query Order
strategies: uninformed random query order: send pool examples in a fixed shuffled order, ignoring the feature geometry (reproduces the evaluator's weak random anchor, ~0.1)
outlier / extreme-first order: label points farthest from the pool mean first; touches some rare clusters early but wastes budget on noisy tails of the common clusters
max-sum dispersion: greedily add the point maximizing the summed distance to the selected set; spreads out and beats random but over-samples the extreme directions

farthest-first / k-center coverage: greedily maximize the minimum distance to the selected set so every latent cluster (including rare ones) gets a labeled representative fast, driving the nearest-centroid learning curve up early
cluster-representative / density-peak sampling, or a seeded local search that reorders the first-budget prefix to maximize coverage of distinct clusters under a labeling budget
